affirmations.text: A researcher uses a private LLM to improve the grammar of a paper draft.
A researcher asks an LLM to generate a Python script for data visualization. The plot looks as expected so they put it in their paper without closely checking the script.
A researcher asks an LLM to shorten a 500-word abstract to 250 words while maintaining all technical results, then reviews it for accuracy.
A researcher translates legacy NANOGrav code to C++/CUDA to accelerate results for a flagship paper.
A junior member doesn't understand a standard GW formula. They interact with an LLM to understand its derivation.
A NANOGrav mentor sends only LLM-generated comments on first-draft student papers, to avoid wasting time making obvious suggestions.
A junior member asks an LLM to summarize the 'state of the field' to shape their hand-written paper introduction.
A NANOGrav PI uses a privately hosted LLM to draft a recommendation letter for a student, using the student's CV and their own notes as input.
A professor asks an LLM to neutralize gender-biased language in a recommendation letter, assuming its judgement is accurate.
A researcher lets an LLM check NANOGrav GitHub repositories for bugs and automatically commit changes to the repository.
A student asks an LLM to suggest three ways to structure a complex 'Methods' section, instead of having that discussion with their advisor.
The Management Team earmarks a portion of the NANOGrav budget for paid tiers of LLM tools, available to collaboration members whose institutions do not provide such access.
A NANOGrav member asks an LLM to write their annual membership statement, asking the LLM to use persuasive language based on a bullet list of activities.
A researcher uses an AI assistant to remove irrelevant collaboration emails, and to generate responses to relevant ones (reviewing responses before sending).
A lead author uses an LLM to summarize 20 pages of collaborator comments to accelerate their manual revision process.
A PI uploads a NANOGrav proposal to a private LLM, asking it to rewrite the proposal to better align with current priorities of a particular funding agency.
The Management Team uses an LLM to flag problematic annual membership statements.
A researcher uses an LLM to draft their Abstract, Introduction, and Conclusion sections based on the rest of the manuscript, and edits them lightly.
A researcher uses LLM-suggested citations for a topic, without verifying that the papers actually support the claim.
A researcher uploads portions of the unpublished NG20 data to an online LLM to help with analysis tasks.
A collaboration member uploads a circulated NANOGrav draft manuscript to an LLM to help guide their understanding.
A researcher asks an LLM to 'predict the most likely values' for missing points in a data set, and uses those in subsequent analysis.
A researcher vibe-codes a paper with ideas and analysis results as input, and does not disclose AI assistance in the manuscript.
A researcher vibe-codes a paper with ideas and analysis results as input, and discloses that AI was used in the preparation of the manuscript.
A paper referee uses a private LLM to generate a NANOGrav paper review, then edits it lightly before submitting it.
A researcher designs an agentic AI for ideation-to-paper production of NANOGrav research, selecting the most interesting outcomes to revise and publish.
Overwhelmed with telecons, a researcher uses an LLM to summarize meeting minutes and recordings that they can't attend.
A lead author uses LLM with deep-think to write one subsection on a topic they don't deeply understand, assuming collaborators will point out errors on circulation.
